summary: Iteration 104 - Offline Sync Auto-Sync Bug Fix VERIFIED. The OfflineContext.jsx fix is working correctly: (1) pendingCount added to auto-sync useEffect dependencies, (2) refs used for stable callbacks, (3) exponential backoff retry added, (4) visibilitychange listener added. Backend 9/10 tests passed (1 unrelated REDD stats endpoint bug). Frontend compiles without errors, agent login works, REDD form loads and offline queueing verified.

minor.issue: 500 Internal Server Error - TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'datetime.datetime' and 'str' in redd_tracking.py line 240
minor.note: Pre-existing bug, not related to offline sync fix

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix REDD stats endpoint datetime comparison bug in /app/backend/routes/redd_tracking.py line 240
critical_code_review_comments: OfflineContext.jsx line 173-178: Auto-sync useEffect now depends on [isOnline, pendingCount, doSync] - KEY FIX VERIFIED
OfflineContext.jsx line 55-60: Refs (isOnlineRef, pendingCountRef) used to avoid stale closures in callbacks - VERIFIED
OfflineContext.jsx line 106-115: Exponential backoff retry with max 60s delay - VERIFIED
OfflineContext.jsx line 180-189: visibilitychange listener syncs when user returns to tab - VERIFIED
OfflineContext.jsx line 191-200: queueAction increments pendingCount and triggers immediate sync if online - VERIFIED
REDDTrackingPage.jsx line 200-230: Offline queueing in catch block properly queues redd_visit action - VERIFIED
agent_search.py line 752-796: Backend redd_visit handler stores in redd_tracking_visits with calculated score - VERIFIED

success_rate.backend: 90% (9/10 tests passed - 1 unrelated REDD stats bug)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (OfflineContext compiles, agent login works, REDD form loads, offline queueing verified)

seed_data_creation: Created test REDD sync entries via /api/agent/sync/upload endpoint
context_for_next_testing_agent: Offline sync auto-sync fix verified. The key fix was adding pendingCount to the auto-sync useEffect dependencies in OfflineContext.jsx. This ensures sync triggers when new items are queued, not just when online status changes. The REDD stats endpoint has a pre-existing datetime comparison bug that should be fixed separately.
